mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates during a liquidity crisis reflect deteriorating financial conditions and rising default risk, which directly suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, corporate earnings growth decelerates sharply due to reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >50% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent monthly decline in retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-liquidity-shock recovery phase, a rebound in industrial production indicates stabilizing supply chains and renewed business activity, generating positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation falls below 2.5 percent, the probability of aggressive monetary easing increases, lowering discount rates and supporting positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of periods following 3 consecutive CPIAUCSL declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls reflects weakening economic activity and reduced corporate profitability, which systematically produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth turns negative alongside a monthly drop in retail sales, it confirms a synchronized demand contraction that depresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of negative GDPC1 growth and falling RSAFS yields a positive 60-day return in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a high-volatility crisis regime, an increase in industrial production signals resilient manufacturing output that outweighs financial sector distress, driving positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during high-volatility periods are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations.
